seo.title: The sauce of all sauces – more than just a salad dressing - Marinda Kook

html:
When we lived in PE, we used to go to Dulcies for salad just to chase down the sauce. It took a long time to work out the sauce. Although I call it a salad dressing, it's just as good over steak, over potatoes, for a chicken mayo, over asparagus, with fish, as a dip for chips and honestly just over everything. The sauce really keeps for a long time in the fridge. I gave the sauce today on RSG's Tjailatyd for the listeners.

Here I give the recipe in my friend Anneli's handwriting:

Just remember 1 teaspoon salt. Put everything in the blender and blend for 5 minutes until it looks like a creamy salad dressing. Pour it out into a bottle and store in the fridge. Tonight we ate the sauce over potatoes in their jackets.
